Midsommar (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $27,426,363Details
International Box Office $19,287,068
Worldwide Box Office $46,713,431
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $581,367
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,569,188
Total $3,150,555
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Dani and Christian are a young American couple with a relationship on the brink of falling apart. But after a family tragedy keeps them together, a grieving Dani invites herself to join Christian and his friends on a trip to a once-in-a-lifetime midsummer festival in a remote Swedish village. What begins as a carefree summer holiday in a land of eternal sunlight takes a sinister turn when the insular villagers invite their guests to partake in festivities that render the pastoral paradise increasingly unnerving and viscerally disturbing.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,560,030 (23.9% of total gross)
Legs:4.18 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:58.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$9,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,707 opening theaters/2,707 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $35,809,962

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 3rd, 2019 (Wide) by A24
June 20th, 2024 (IMAX) by A24 (Director’s Cut)
International Releases: July 5th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 5th, 2019 (Wide) (Poland)
July 5th, 2019 (Wide), released as Saulės kultas (Lithuania)
July 11th, 2019 (Wide) (South Korea)
July 12th, 2019 (Wide) (Taiwan)
Video Release: September 24th, 2019 by A24
MPA Rating: R for disturbing ritualistic violence and grisly images, strong sexual content, graphic nudity, drug use and language.
(Rating bulletin 2584 (Cert #52245), 6/26/2019)
Running Time: 147 minutes
Geographical setting: Sweden
Plot point: Death of a Sibling, Family Vacations, Mental Illness, Murder - Suicide, Narcotics, Occult, Relationships Gone Wrong, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Subgenre: Psychological Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Square Peg, B-Reel Films, A24
Production Countries: United States, Sweden
Languages: English, Swedish

Synopsis

Dani and Christian are a young American couple with a relationship on the brink of falling apart. But after a family tragedy keeps them together, a grieving Dani invites herself to join Christian and his friends on a trip to a once-in-a-lifetime midsummer festival in a remote Swedish village. What begins as a carefree summer holiday in a land of eternal sunlight takes a sinister turn when the insular villagers invite their guests to partake in festivities that render the pastoral paradise increasingly unnerving and viscerally disturbing.
